x-enumeration-literals.name: Instant Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rewards are applied immediately when the transaction occurs.

Comment:
Example: Using points instantly to pay at a partner merchant.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Scheduled Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rewards can be used at a future date or scheduled for redemption.

Comment:
Example: Scheduling points to pay a recurring bill next month.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Event-Based Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rewards can be redeemed only when a specific event occurs.

Comment:
Example: Redeem points only during a promotional campaign.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Expiry Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rewards must be burned before they expire.

Comment:
Example: Points expiring at the end of the year must be used before that date.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Conditional Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Rewards can be redeemed only if certain conditions are met or approval is given.

Comment:
Example: Points can be applied only if the transaction exceeds a minimum value.
